title: Interview outfit
summary: A safe, neat outfit for an interview or recruitment meeting.
steps: Choose a shirt, plain blouse or simple sweater.
Add chinos, dark clean jeans, straight trousers or a simple skirt.
Shoes should be clean, calm and comfortable.
warnings: The outfit should support the conversation, not distract from it.
dontDo: do not judge the body
do not choose too many loud items

title: Interview — full exit checklist
steps: Base: shirt, plain blouse or simple sweater in a calm color.
Bottom: chinos, straight trousers, dark clean jeans or classic skirt.
Layer: blazer, cardigan or clean light jacket if weather requires it.
Shoes: clean loafers, ankle boots, simple dress shoes or minimal sneakers.
Accessories: one stronger accent at most, keep the rest neutral.
Before leaving: check shoes, creases and weather.
dontDo: do not judge attractiveness
do not wear items that disturb sitting

title: Smart casual meeting
summary: A neat but not overly formal meeting outfit.
steps: Combine a simple top with chinos or dark jeans.
Add a blazer, cardigan or clean light jacket.
Choose shoes suitable for the place.
warnings: Match formality to the people and location.
dontDo: do not mix too many patterns

title: Meeting — smart casual variant
summary: A practical outfit for a business or semi-formal meeting.
steps: Top: plain shirt, knitwear or blouse without large prints.
Bottom: chinos, straight trousers, dark jeans or skirt.
Layer: blazer or cardigan when you need more formality.
Shoes: loafers, clean sneakers, ankle boots or simple dress shoes.
Colors: keep to 2–3 colors and one accent.
Checklist: phone, documents, weather, clean cuffs and collar.
dontDo: do not build the outfit around body criticism
do not choose pieces you must constantly adjust

title: School or university
summary: Comfortable outfit for studying and moving around.
steps: Choose a T-shirt, hoodie, sweater or casual shirt.
Add jeans, chinos, skirt or comfortable trousers.
Pick comfortable shoes and a layer for weather changes.
dontDo: do not wear new shoes for a long day

title: School or university — all-day outfit
summary: Comfortable outfit with weather and bag planning.
steps: Base: T-shirt, blouse, casual shirt or thin sweater.
Bottom: jeans, chinos, skirt or trousers comfortable for sitting.
Layer: hoodie, cardigan or light jacket you can remove indoors.
Shoes: sneakers, ankle boots or other tested walking shoes.
Accessory: backpack or bag that does not reduce comfort.
Checklist: weather, charger, documents, clean shoes.
dontDo: do not dress only for a photo
do not wear pieces uncomfortable for sitting

title: Party or night out
summary: One stronger accent with a comfortable base.
steps: Choose one standout element: color, texture or accessory.
Keep the rest of the outfit simpler.
Choose shoes you can actually wear for the whole outing.
warnings: One strong accent works better than everything at once.
dontDo: do not choose shoes that hurt after 10 minutes

title: Party — polished variant
steps: Base: simple shirt, blouse, dress, top or knitwear.
Accent: one stronger color, shine, texture or accessory.
Bottom: straight trousers, clean jeans, skirt or dress.
Shoes: choose what you can walk and stand in.
Layer: jacket or blazer suitable for arrival and return.
Checklist: pockets or small bag, weather, freedom of movement.
dontDo: do not judge the body
do not wear every strong item at once

title: Walk and weather
steps: Start with comfortable shoes.
Add a weather-dependent layer.
Choose clothes that do not restrict movement.
warnings: For a walk, comfort beats formality.
dontDo: do not ignore rain or wind

title: Walk — practical outfit
summary: Outfit for walking, weather and errands.
steps: Shoes: sneakers, city trekking shoes, ankle boots or tested footwear.
Base: T-shirt, sweater or hoodie depending on temperature.
Bottom: jeans, chinos or comfortable trousers.
Layer: rain jacket, coat or light hoodie.
Accessories: umbrella, hat, scarf or sunglasses depending on weather.
Checklist: pockets, phone, keys and hourly weather.
dontDo: do not choose slippery shoes for rain
do not wear stiff clothing for a long walk

title: Family occasion
summary: Neat, calm outfit without overdoing it.
steps: Choose a clean, ironed top.
Add a simple bottom and calm shoes.
Use neutral colors or one soft accent.
warnings: Family occasions usually work well with calm neatness.
dontDo: do not go too sporty if the occasion is formal

title: Family occasion — neat set
summary: Safe option for dinner, holiday or ceremony.
steps: Top: shirt, blouse, sweater or dress in a calm color.
Bottom: straight trousers, chinos, skirt or dress.
Layer: blazer, cardigan or coat depending on location.
Shoes: clean and comfortable, more neat than sporty.
Accessories: one delicate accent is enough.
Checklist: weather, family photos, comfort while sitting.
dontDo: do not judge the person
do not wear something impossible to sit in comfortably

title: Rain-ready variant
steps: Choose moisture-resistant shoes.
Add a jacket, coat or umbrella.
Avoid long hems touching wet pavement.
warnings: Rain can easily ruin shoes and the lower part of the outfit.
dontDo: do not wear delicate shoes in heavy rain

title: Rain — practical checklist
steps: Shoes: choose a pair that handles moisture and does not slip.
Bottom: avoid fabric that pulls water from the pavement.
Top: choose a layer that still looks good after removing the jacket.
Protection: umbrella, hood or light raincoat.
Bag: choose one that rain will not damage.
After arrival: check hems, hair and shoes.
dontDo: do not ignore slippery soles
do not dress only for the indoor part

title: Cold weather layers
summary: Outfit you can adjust between cold and warm spaces.
steps: Wear a base, warm layer and outer layer.
Match shoes to temperature.
Add scarf, hat or gloves if needed.
warnings: It is better to remove a layer than to be cold.
dontDo: do not rely on one thick item with no adjustment

title: Cold — layered outfit
summary: Cold-weather outfit that still looks consistent.
steps: Layer 1: T-shirt, thermal top or thin blouse.
Layer 2: sweater, hoodie, cardigan or knit blazer.
Layer 3: jacket, coat or parka.
Bottom: material suitable for temperature and wind.
Shoes: warmer sneakers, ankle boots or boots according to occasion.
Accessories: hat, scarf or gloves matching the outfit color.
dontDo: do not ignore wind
do not choose shoes only by appearance

title: Simple outfit plan
summary: Universal proposal when you do not know where to start.
steps: Choose one simple base.
Match the bottom to the occasion.
Check weather and shoes.
warnings: Start with occasion and weather, then accessories.
dontDo: do not judge yourself, judge only outfit fit to situation

title: Universal outfit method
summary: Short method for building an outfit for any occasion.
steps: Occasion: define formality — casual, neat or elegant.
Weather: add or remove a layer.
Base: choose a top that does not conflict with the occasion.
Bottom: choose color and material calmer than the top, or the reverse.
Shoes: they must fit the place and walking time.
Checklist: cleanliness, weather, sitting comfort, pockets or bag.
dontDo: do not build the outfit from body criticism
do not skip weather
